message:
fix(security): prevent XSS-through-DOM and stack trace exposure (#9226)

* fix(security): prevent XSS-through-DOM and stack trace exposure (Fixes #9219)

IframeEmbed.tsx: stop reading iframeRef.current.src back from the DOM
before reassigning it — that DOM-read → DOM-write pattern is the
XSS-through-DOM sink CodeQL flags (alert 182). Use the sanitized `url`
state variable directly, which already passed through sanitizeIframeUrl().

Netlify functions: remove detail: String(err) from all error response
bodies in nps.mts (alerts 494, 495), bonus-points.mts (alert 493), and
youtube-playlist.mts (alert 262). Stack traces / error messages must not
leak to clients. Log errors server-side with console.error() instead and
return a generic "Internal server error" message.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(security): break js/xss-through-dom taint in IframeEmbed (Fixes #9219)

- sanitizeIframeUrl() now returns parsed.href instead of the raw input,
  breaking CodeQL's taint chain at the URL parse boundary
- Apply sanitizeIframeUrl() at the DOM sink (iframeRef.current.src) rather
  than relying on pre-sanitised state, so CodeQL can see the sanitizer
  directly at the sink

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
